lint.note: Pre-existing lint failures unrelated to docs-cleanup changes: golangci-lint binary (go1.24) is incompatible with go1.26 stdlib. All errors are in sentryhook (undefined sentry), third-party modules (otel, grpc), and pre-existing composite literal issues in test files. Verified identical lint output on HEAD and HEAD~2.

blockingIssues.description: All API response examples across docs/openclaw-manifest.json, docs/api-reference.md, and docs/error-codes.md show meta.mode: 'json', but the API server (internal/server/auth.go) returns mode: 'api'. CLI --json mode uses 'json' but API endpoints use 'api'. Every envelope example in the docs is wrong for API usage.

Health endpoint docs describe ffmpeg.version field (e.g., 'n8.1') but checkFFmpegStatus() in server.go only returns {status: 'available'|'not_found'} with no version field. Also documents ffmpeg status value 'unavailable' but code returns 'not_found'.
Health endpoint docs describe upstream.error field but checkUpstreamStatus() in server.go only returns {status: 'reachable'|'unreachable'} with no error field.

appliedUpdates.description: Updated architecture.md health endpoint section to document exact field names and values: config returns {status, hasUsername, hasPassword, hasBaseUrl}, upstream returns {status: 'reachable'|'unreachable'} with no error field, ffmpeg returns {status: 'available'|'not_found'} with no version field.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add a note in the documentation section about the meta.mode distinction: API server returns mode: 'api', CLI --json returns mode: 'json'. Workers updating docs should check which surface they are documenting.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: update-all-docs review found all API docs incorrectly using mode: 'json'. The mode distinction is documented in architecture.md but not in AGENTS.md guidance for doc writers.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Remove the reference to 'internal/config/feature_flags.go - Feature flags' from the key files list (line ~43), since this file has been deleted.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: cleanup-feature-flags review found stale reference to deleted file in skill documentation.

rejectedObservations.observation: docs/observability.md references 8 IMPARTUS_FLAG_* environment variables that no longer work after feature flag removal.
rejectedObservations.reason: This is a documentation accuracy issue within the scope of a potential fix feature, not a shared state observation for the library or services.yaml. The orchestrator can decide whether to create a follow-up task.
